name: Jasmine Moonbeam
description: This tool models ground-level particulate matter (PM) concentrations downwind of a cement kiln using a simplified Gaussian dispersion approach, comparing three engineering control alternatives against an uncontrolled baseline. The user adjusts four inputs — stack height (H), wind speed (u), atmospheric stability class (A–F), and which alternatives to display — and the tool outputs a concentration vs. distance profile from 100 m to 10 km, peak concentration, receptor-point concentration at 1,500 m, and the downwind distance at which each alternative achieves EPA NAAQS PM₂.₅ compliance (9 µg/m³). The Gaussian model was selected because the cement kiln represents a continuous elevated point source in open terrain, well-suited to the Pasquill-Gifford dispersion framework. The only variable that changes between alternatives is the emission rate Q (g/s), which applies each device's rated removal efficiency to the uncontrolled kiln emission of 75 g/s. Outputs represent worst-case centerline ground-level concentrations under steady-state conditions — real concentrations will vary with wind direction, terrain, and equipment aging.
